snippet.title: Tales from Dollar Tree Hell | A Manager speaks
snippet.description:
#DollarTree #retail #podcast

Tonight “Blank Stares” joins us to discuss the hell of working for Dollar Tree

Intro: 00:00-02:01
Headlines(Dollar Tree Edition): 02:01-04:32
Sponsor: 04:32-04:54
Dollar Tree Manager: 04:54-52:19
Question of the Week(Dollar Store Edition): 52:19-57:00
Closing/Thanks for Watching: 57:00-1:05:00

Our Sponsor: The Serving Times https://theservingtimes.wordpress.com/

️ “Please Don’t Be a Karen This Year” – https://youtu.be/n4QrKqw9mGU

Tonight’s Headlines:
https://www.retaildive.com/news/dollar-tree-names-former-dollar-general-exec-rick-dreiling-ceo/641059/
https://www.10tv.com/article/news/local/family-and-friends-honor-dollar-tree-employee-killed-in-machete-attack/530-bfc9299c-c6f8-4383-a365-312e2fc3bc0b
https://www.kens5.com/article/news/community/dollar-tree-noise-machine-homeless-san-antonio/273-4273133f-9a7c-49a8-b351-8e0cfbdc9df7
https://www.wesh.com/article/dollar-tree-armed-robbery-daytona-beach/42595403
https://thetandd.com/news/local/crime-and-courts/report-1-700-taken-following-card-game-at-orangeburg-dollar-tree/article_587e982d-6be2-563d-9d15-7889a49150d1.html
https://weartv.com/news/local/santa-rosa-county-sheriffs-office-responds-to-gulf-breeze-dollar-tree-incident
https://www.elpasotimes.com/story/news/crime/2022/04/17/el-paso-robbery-punches-dollar-tree-store-crime-week/7351022001/
https://www.nbc29.com/2022/12/08/police-4-shot-dollar-tree-new-orleans/
https://douglasnow.com/index.php/news/item/10901-former-dollar-tree-manager-arrested-for-allegedly-stealing-over-31-000
https://www.osha.gov/news/newsreleases/region5/12012022
https://www.osha.gov/news/newsreleases/region5/08012022


We all know how frustrating working in the retail/grocery and service industry can be. From low wages to ridiculous job expectations we are here to shine a light on the horrors that exist after you punch the clock.

//Recommended videos:
️ The Traits of a Toxic Boss – https://youtu.be/pasMOQFVxM8
️ Justice for Evan | The organizers speak – https://youtu.be/LDyXD5OmAGI
️ An Evening with Senator Jeff Brandes of Florida – https://youtu.be/r7acO4wozEc
️ Mental Health in Retail | Why your stories matter – https://youtu.be/c2iWxR1pNHE


//Let’s Connect:
 All of our links in one place: https://linktr.ee/TheRetailWarzone
 Twitter: https://twitter.com/GenerationVoid
 Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/TheRetailWarzone
 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/theretailwarzone/
 Twitch: https://www.twitch.tv/theretailwarzone
 Email: thegenerationvoid@gmail.com

//About Us:
Our main goal here is to be a voice for the underappreciated and oftentimes abused retail worker. We are a community of retail veterans and have a true grasp of what the daily abuses each of you suffer at the hands of overbearing management and customers. This is more than just a Youtube channel for us, this is meant to be a community that we want to grow into a juggernaut that maybe could help institute some change. Needless to say, we are glad you are here!

snippet.title: Investor Nightmare: Dollar Tree Stock Tumbles after Earnings
snippet.description:
#dltrstocknews #dltrstockanalysis #dltrstocktoday

Dollar Tree suffered another earnings miss on Wednesday, and the stock fell as a result.  Dollar Tree (DLTR) announced Thursday that it is fighting shoplifting, or "shrink" as the industry calls it.

On Thursday, the discount retailer's stock fell more than 12% after reporting a weaker-than-expected projection and lower gross margins due to a customer shift toward consumables and "elevated shrink."

The company's current quarter gross margins of 29.2% fell 220 basis points from last year and fell short of consensus projections of 29.7%. Dollar Tree's quarterly profit of $0.91 per share topped $0.87 forecasts. The business expected to earn $0.94-$1.09 per share in the third quarter, behind Wall Street's $1.29.

Dollar Tree's bottom line is also hurting as customers spend more on lower-margin perishables. "You see across the retail landscape, people are moving more into consumables and we're not immune," Davis said. "We are now taking a very defensive approach to shrink," Dollar Tree CEO Rick Dreiling said on the earnings call. "I don't mind reaching more realistic margins."
